prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident involving the 'svc_etl' account on host 10.20.3.15 has been detected. Over 1.1 GB of data, including sensitive FINANCE_PII from '/data/finance_q2.zip', was uploaded to the suspicious external domain 'upload.evil-cdn.net'. Multiple security systems confirmed this as a data exfiltration pattern, aligning with MITRE T1041.

timeline.event: User 'svc_etl' from 10.20.3.15 initiated a connection to upload.evil-cdn.net:443.
User 'svc_etl' uploaded an additional 612MB to upload.evil-cdn.net/bucket/xfer.
Firewall detected an outbound anomaly: 10.20.3.15 sent 1.1GB to 185.220.101.44 in 77 seconds.
DLP detected sensitive label FINANCE_PII in outbound transfer by 'svc_etl'.
SIEM detected a data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to rare external domain.
IDS suspected MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host 10.20.3.15.
Proxy observed TLS SNI for upload.evil-cdn.net from client 'svc_etl'.
Netflow detected a volume spike (14x baseline) from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
SIEM issued a CRITICAL alert for data_exfiltration by user 'svc_etl' to 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.
EDR reported process 'curl' uploading '/data/finance_q2.zip' initiated by 'svc_etl'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data loss or compromise.
Disable or reset credentials for the 'svc_etl' account and investigate its activity for signs of compromise.
Initiate a full forensic investigation on host 10.20.3.15 to determine the initial compromise vector and scope of the breach.
Block all communication to 'upload.evil-cdn.net' and IP address 185.220.101.44 at the network perimeter.
Assess the impact of the exfiltrated FINANCE_PII and begin incident response procedures, including potential regulatory notification.
